relatability_triggers: Over the next few pages, I'm going to reveal how I went from being arguably the 'Worst Investor in America' (I'm totally serious. I lost almost ALL my life savings in just 45 days... and nearly ruined my marriage!)... To getting it all back PLUS $309,000-in just 48 months.

s_specific_solution.core_mechanism: The Magic Calculator (based on Secret Mathematical Formula): Without giving you the exact formula just yet, the Magic Calculator gives you a precise way to TIME your investments. As I'm sure you've seen in your own experiences, knowing which investments to buy is actually only a small part of the equation. Knowing WHEN to buy and WHEN to sell is even more important. What my Magic Calculator does is show you a PRECISE way to time every trade-as mathematically close to perfection as possible. I'm talking about getting out of losing trades very quickly so that your losers always remain very small... and staying in winning trades to the point of maximum gains. Believe it or not, the Magic Calculator will tell you exactly which price to sell! The formula was scribbled on a cocktail napkin by a rich former hedge fund manager - a PhD in finance who worked for two billion-dollar hedge funds. He said: 'This formula is one of the most important secrets you'll ever learn as an investor. It is more important than what stocks you buy, how you invest your money, or which way the market moves.'

headline.main_headline: Self-Proclaimed: Worst Investor in America Invents 'Magic' Calculator to Get Back Everything He Lost PLUS $309,000 in Just 48 Months
headline.subheadline: Try this 'Magic Calculator' for free on your own and see how much more money you make--even if you never buy a new stock, ever again.
specificity_elements: even if you never buy a new stock, ever again
headline.promise_compression: Worst investor → Magic Calculator → $309,000 profit + all losses recovered

curiosity_architecture.gap_created: How could the worst investor create something that recovers losses and makes profits?
curiosity_architecture.specificity_depth: Specific dollar amount ($309,000), specific timeframe (48 months), paradoxical identity (worst investor inventing something)
curiosity_architecture.resolution_promise: Try it free, see results without buying new stocks

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: The worst investor should stay the worst investor - they shouldn't be qualified to create investing tools or teach others. Success should come from expertise, not from failure.
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Failure-to-expertise transformation in a space dominated by expert gurus; mathematical formula that makes stock picking irrelevant; admission of incompetence as credibility anchor

emotional_engineering.progression: shame identification → curiosity → hope → greed → vindication

headline.vsl_adaptation: Perfect for VSL - open with shame/failure story, reveal mathematical breakthrough, demonstrate results on average investors

lead.full_text: Dear Reader, Hi, my name is Dr. R. Smith. Over the next few pages, I'm going to reveal how I went from being arguably the 'Worst Investor in America' (I'm totally serious. I lost almost ALL my life savings in just 45 days... and nearly ruined my marriage!)... To getting it all back PLUS $309,000-in just 48 months. The secret behind my big turnaround? Well, believe it or not, I stumbled across a huge mathematical secret... and I created what many people in the financial industry now refer to as a 'Magic' Calculator. Now, I know this sounds pretty crazy. But despite being a bad investor, I've been a math whiz my entire life. That's how I got into Berkeley for college and that's why some of the biggest companies in America (like Pfizer and Johnson & Johnson) hired me to crunch their numbers.
lead.hook_sentence: Over the next few pages, I'm going to reveal how I went from being arguably the 'Worst Investor in America' (I'm totally serious. I lost almost ALL my life savings in just 45 days... and nearly ruined my marriage!)... To getting it all back PLUS $309,000-in just 48 months.
lead.transition_to_body: The secret behind my big turnaround?

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: Confessing incompetence as an opening move (vs. establishing expertise); math genius who can't invest (contradiction); failure as qualification rather than disqualification
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Vulnerability-as-authority; mathematical intelligence separated from investing intelligence; transformation narrative front-loaded

Stay in winning trades to the point of maximum gains
Increase investment gains by 50% or even 100% each year

Remove emotion and primal brain responses from investing decisions
Outperform even professional analysts and famous investors
Make money even if you pick stocks that go up only 50-60% of the time
promise_architecture.promise_ceiling: 1,379% returns (David Ryan), 19,900% returns (John Templeton), 2,700% returns (Peter Lynch), 30% per year for 20 years (Steve Cohen), turn $3.5M into $7.3M (famous TV investor improved)
promise_architecture.promise_floor: Stop losing money on bad timing, recover losses from past mistakes, never suffer catastrophic losses like Enron/Tyco/WorldCom

proof_pairings.Dr. R. Smith: $309,000 profit in 48 months after losing everything
proof_pairings.Verizon: Over 100% gain, still holding years later
From -23% losses to +56% gains on SAME stocks
From -50% losses to +54% gains on SAME stocks
proof_pairings.Ken H. Wisconsin: 248% on EPD, never lost a penny since

promise_architecture.promise_repetition: Very high - transformation claim in headline, repeated in lead, demonstrated in stories, proven in case studies, validated in professional testing
promise_architecture.failure_reframe: You're not bad at picking stocks - you're bad at TIMING those stocks. Even the best investors only pick winners 50-60% of the time. The difference is they optimize exits. Your brain isn't wired to make optimal timing decisions - you need the mathematical formula to override emotion.

three_part_structure.what_they_think_problem_is: I'm bad at picking stocks. I choose the wrong investments. I need to find better stock picks or get better investment advice.
three_part_structure.what_real_problem_is: The human brain is not 'wired' to make optimal investment timing decisions. The same part of your brain that processes life-threatening danger (telling you to run from lions/bears) ALSO processes investment decisions about buying and selling. This causes you to make decisions based on raw emotion rather than logic or probability. You buy high (when excitement peaks) and sell low (when panic peaks). Even expert stock pickers only win 50-60% of the time - but they OPTIMIZE timing to maximize winners and minimize losers using mathematical formulas.
three_part_structure.why_nothing_worked: Books, seminars, wealth coaches, and brokerage houses all focus on PICKING better stocks. But stock selection is only a small part of the equation. The real secret is TIMING - knowing exactly when to sell. Without a mathematical formula to override your primal brain responses, you'll keep selling winners too early (to lock in gains) and holding losers too long (hoping they'll recover). Studies prove investors are 1.5x more likely to sell winners than losers, and twice as likely to hold losing positions.

villain_profile.primary_villain: Your own brain - specifically the primal fear/greed circuitry that hijacks rational decision-making
villain_profile.villain_quote: Your investing brain often drives you to do things that make no logical sense-but make perfect emotional sense. Your brain developed to improve our species' odds of survival. You, like every other human, are wired to crave what looks rewarding and shun what seems risky. To counteract these impulses your brain has only a thin veneer of modern, analytical circuits that are often no match for the power of the ancient parts of your mind.
villain_profile.secondary_villain: The investment industry that teaches stock selection instead of timing optimization

addressed: Too good to be true → Used by billionaire traders (Paul Tudor Jones, Michael Marcus), employees at Bank of America/Wells Fargo/UBS/Morgan Stanley
Need better stock picks → No, even legendary investors (Templeton, Lynch, Cohen, Ryan) only pick winners 50-60% of the time. Timing is what matters.
Need to be smart investor → No, Craig lost 23% becomes +56%, Trevor lost 50% becomes +54% on SAME stocks with calculator
Have to change investments → No, just optimize TIMING on what you already own
Complicated system → Takes minutes to set up, automated alerts via email/text
Need to watch market → Set it and forget it - calculator monitors everything daily

root_cause_architecture.repetition_pattern: Introduced via personal failure story, explained via neuroscience research (NIH studies), reinforced through case studies showing SAME stocks producing opposite results based on timing, validated through professional analyst testing

narrative_reorganization.self_blame_removal: Your brain is literally not wired for optimal investment timing. The National Institutes of Health proved that the same brain region processing life-threatening danger ALSO processes investment decisions. You're fighting millions of years of evolution telling you to run from danger (sell winners to lock in gains) and freeze when trapped (hold losers hoping for recovery). This isn't a character flaw - it's neurobiology. Even expert investors only pick winners 50-60% of the time. You weren't failing because you're stupid - you were failing because you're human.
narrative_reorganization.coherence_creation: All these failures connect: buying Independence Air and riding it to bankruptcy, selling Autonomy too early and missing 280% gains, panic-selling during crashes, holding losers too long, getting paralyzed by fear, following the crowd, losing to Enron/Tyco/WorldCom - ALL stem from using your primal brain (designed for physical survival) to make timing decisions (requiring mathematical optimization). One formula fixes everything.
narrative_reorganization.new_self_concept: From 'worst investor who can't pick stocks' to 'mathematician who uses proven formula to optimize timing like legendary investors do - making money even when picking stocks that only go up 50% of the time'

specimens.quote: Over the next few pages, I'm going to reveal how I went from being arguably the 'Worst Investor in America' (I'm totally serious. I lost almost ALL my life savings in just 45 days... and nearly ruined my marriage!)... To getting it all back PLUS $309,000-in just 48 months.

One stock I tried it out on was a beverage company called Constellation Brands. I'd gotten in at $18.79 per share... Over the next two months I was stunned as I watched Constellation shoot past $30... then $35... $40 a share! Then finally, when the share price hit $65.26 the calculator told me it was time to 'sell.' And I gladly did, for an incredible 247% gain.

The next stock I entered into the Calculator was a small biotech company called Sangamo Biosciences. I had gotten in at $5.88 a share... Then, finally, at $17.43 it told me to 'sell'-a 196% gain! I couldn't believe it. Two trades. Two unbelievable gains.

I purchased shares of Verizon, the popular wireless service provider, at $23 a share... It would eventually go to over $50 per share... and guess what? I'm still in the trade today-several years later! This has turned out to potentially be a legacy investment for my family, which I plan on passing down to my children and grandchildren.

In 2006, I bought shares of a small technology company called Immersion Corp for $5.66 a share... at $16.11 the calculator told me to 'get out' of the stock... I sold it for an incredible 185% gain... over the course of the next several months - after I'd already sold - Immersion Corp. steadily fell to less than $3 per share... well BELOW my original entry point! If I didn't have the Magic Calculator to tell me WHEN to exit the position... who knows when I would have actually sold shares!

Craig T., a 60-year-old retired psychologist... Between 2010 and 2012, Craig's stock picks lost 23% of their value. The stock market, by comparison, went up about 37% over the same period... But if he'd used the Magic Calculator, Craig wouldn't have lost anything at all. In fact, his picks would have GAINED more than 56% over the same period... These were the EXACT same stocks... over the EXACT same period. AND he would have beaten the market.

One of the stocks Craig invested in was Constellation Brands... Craig bought shares in early 2012 for $20.73 per share... He sold a few months later for a 6.8% loss... But what if Craig had used the Magic Calculator instead? Well, the Magic Calculator would have kept Craig in the trade... he'd still be in the trade today... up over 200%! In other words, without my Magic Calculator Craig managed a 6.8% loss. With it, he could have made more than 200%... on the very same stock.

Another trade I found in Craig's portfolio was Eastman Kodak. He purchased shares in August 2011 for $2.69 a share... Craig finally sold at 30 cents a share... for an 89% LOSS. But get this... If he had used the Magic Calculator he could have gotten out much sooner... The Magic Calculator would have told him to 'get out' here - just a few weeks after his initial buy - for a small loss of only 5%... instead of losing nearly 90% of his money.

David P. is a retired dentist from Maclean, Virginia... Over the past six years, Dave's stock picks gained 69%. That's pretty good considering the stock market went up only about 21% over the same time. But could the Magic Calculator improve Dave's already good performance?... overall Dave's picks gained about 69% between 2007 and now... But using the Magic Calculator could have boosted that number... to over 194%!

Trevor H... Trevor is a retired physical therapist from San Francisco. Just about every investment Trevor has made has gone wrong... horribly wrong... In total, Trevor's stock picks have lost 50% of their value since 2007... But get this... If Trevor had used my Magic Calculator he wouldn't have lost ANY money at all. In fact, he would have actually GAINED 54% over the same period AND beaten the market in the process!

I got in touch with one of the biggest independent financial research firms in America - a group I have a close business relationship with. Today, this $100 million company provides research and recommendations to individual investors in more than 120 countries... One is a PhD and former international mutual fund Vice President whose recommended portfolio has increased 144% over the past decade... Using the Magic Calculator however - on the exact same trades - would have increased that gain to 204%.

The second is a popular value analyst who provides financial research to people at some of the biggest and most well-known banks, brokers, and hedge funds in America- places like JP Morgan Chase, Morgan Stanley, and Charles Schwab. His recommended portfolio has gone up 166% over the past decade... But using the Magic Calculator and the analysts investment ideas together would have made a lot more... 253%.

The third is one of the most widely read financial newsletter writers in America. His recommended portfolio has gone up 300% over the past decade... But using his recommendations together with the Magic Calculator would have made more... 364%.

He's a famous hedge fund guy who you've probably seen on TV. I'm almost certain you'd recognize his name. He has his own cable show... This man's buys and sells turned $3.5 million into $5 million over 12 years. That's pretty good. However, using my Magic Calculator - and the exact same stocks over the exact same time - that number would have grown even bigger... to over $7.3 million. That's 250% more profit, simply by using my Magic Calculator.

Did you know, for example, that Sir John Templeton- 'the greatest stock-picker of the century' according to Money magazine - made money on less than 66% of his trades? But he was able to show investors a 19,900% total return over 38 years, no doubt in part with the help of the magic formula.

Peter Lynch, one of the best and most popular investors of all time, earned an amazing 2,700% over 13 years in his Magellan Fund with the help of the Magic Formula. Peter says even the best investors on the planet are only able to find winning ideas about 60% of the time.

Billionaire hedge fund manager Steve Cohen makes money less often than that! His fund typically only makes money on about 55% of its investments. But with some help from the magic formula I've been describing he's been able to return an unbelievable 30% per year for the last 20 years to investors.

Legendary stock investor David Ryan used the magic formula to win the U.S. Investing Championship THREE times- with a remarkable 3-year return of 1,379%. And he did it by making money on just 50% of his trades!
According to a Bloomberg study of 88,000 investors, for example, 'people are one-and-a half times more likely to sell a winning stock than a losing stock.'

A study of 10,000 investors by the University of California similarly finds that the two most common mistakes investors make is that they 'disproportionately hold onto their losing investments and sell their winners too early.'

In November 2006, universities from around the world jointly analyzed all trading activity on the Taiwan Stock Exchange (TSE) and found that investors are about 'twice as likely to sell a stock if they are holding that stock for a gain rather than a loss.'

Researchers at the National Institutes of Health, for example, recently concluded that the same part of our brain that processes life-threatening danger-the part that tells us to run away from say, a lion or a bear-ALSO processes our decisions about buying and selling investments.

I Made Over $891,000 - 'The Magic Calculator has not changed my approach to investing. It lets me do what I have been doing for 45 years more easily. I have made over $891,000 since I started using it- mostly by doing nothing.' -John. C., Utah

A 248% Gain! - 'I have not lost a penny since using the Magic Calculator. Without it, I would have never seen gains like 172% on MO, 248% on EPD, 147% on KMP, and 111% on APU. I am much better off with your service. Thank you!' -Ken H., Wisconsin

Averaging 20% Per Year - 'I no longer fear the market because the Magic Calculator watches it for me! It tells me when to dump my laggards and let the winners ride! I have been a customer for three years and my annual rate of return has averaged 20%!' -Bhaun P., Santa Monica, CA

proof.strongest_proof_element: Craig T. case study: Same exact stocks, same exact period: -23% without calculator vs +56% with calculator, beating the market. Proves mechanism (timing) is more important than stock selection.
proof.proof_sequence: Personal transformation failure story → First winning trades with calculator → Mechanism explanation → Real-world portfolio testing (Craig/Dave/Trevor) → Professional analyst testing → Famous TV investor testing → Legendary investor validation → Academic research validation → Subscriber testimonials

guarantee.language: I'd like you to take the next FOUR (4) MONTHS to decide if the Magic Calculator and True Wealth are right for you. That should give you plenty of time to see how they both work. If you decide True Wealth or the Magic Calculator aren't right for you, just let us know within the first 4 months, and we'll send you a FULL refund. And you can keep everything you've received up until that point. (After 4 months, you can still change your mind, and receive a pro-rated refund on the remaining portion of your subscriptions.)

offer.risk_reversal_language: Try the Magic Calculator and Dr. Sjuggerud's work for yourself, at no risk. We're asking you only to TRY the Magic Calculator and True Wealth, to see if they are right for you.

fssit_candidates.statement: For most purposes in daily life, your brain is a superbly functioning machine, steering you away from danger while guiding you towards basic rewards like food, shelter, and love. But that brilliant machine can lead you astray when it comes to investing... your investing brain often drives you to do things that make no logical sense-but make perfect emotional sense.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains every irrational investing mistake as neurological rather than personal failure. Your brain is designed for physical survival (running from predators) not financial optimization (holding winners/selling losers). The same brain region processing 'lion chasing you' ALSO processes 'stock dropping' - so you panic-sell winners to lock in gains (escape the lion) and freeze on losers hoping they recover (play dead until danger passes). This isn't stupidity - it's hardwired survival programming betraying you in a domain it wasn't designed for.

fssit_candidates.statement: Ask anyone what the most common mistake investors make is-why most people don't make money in the stock market and- 99 times out of 100- they'll tell you it's that they make the 'bad' investments. This simply is NOT true. Most people don't make money not because they make bad investments... it's because they TRADE those investment the wrong way.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains why all the stock-picking advice failed. You've been solving the wrong problem. The industry taught you to find better stocks, but that was never the issue. Even legendary investors (Templeton, Lynch, Cohen, Ryan) only pick winners 50-60% of the time. They got rich on TIMING optimization, not stock selection. Your entire investing education focused on the 40% of success (stock picking) while ignoring the 60% (timing). That's why nothing worked.

fssit_candidates.statement: I went from being arguably the 'Worst Investor in America' (I'm totally serious. I lost almost ALL my life savings in just 45 days... and nearly ruined my marriage!)... To getting it all back PLUS $309,000-in just 48 months.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: If the worst investor in America can recover everything and make $309,000 profit, your failures aren't permanent character flaws - they're solvable technical problems. The fact that someone who lost EVERYTHING in 45 days can become successful means there's a specific fix (not requiring talent/expertise) that changes outcomes regardless of starting competence level.

rsf_analysis.fssit_placement_pattern: Primary FSSIT in opening (worst investor transformation), amplified by neuroscience revelation (brain not wired for timing), reinforced by reframe (stock picking vs timing), validated throughout case studies (same stocks, opposite results based on timing)

temporal_analysis.cultural_moment_reference: Post-Enron/WorldCom/Tyco scandals (2001-2002), investor distrust of corporate America, emphasis on avoiding catastrophic losses, general market fear from accounting fraud revelations
temporal_analysis.temporal_urgency_device: Recent corporate scandals creating urgency to avoid next disaster, current market opportunity ('I'm eyeballing a few trades now')
evergreen_elements: Brain hardwired for survival not investing (evergreen neuroscience)
Emotional vs logical decision making (timeless psychology)
Timing more important than selection (timeless trading principle)
Even experts pick winners only 50-60% of time (evergreen insight)
Academic research on investor behavior (studies remain valid)
Before/after case studies showing timing impact (evergreen proof structure)
